name: Coquilles St. Jacques
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt 1 tablespoon butter in a heavy skillet and sauté mushrooms. Remove the mushrooms and set aside.
2. Add the remaining 3 tablespoons butter and flour, cooking together over medium heat for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
3. Combine white wine, water, parsley, peppercorns, thyme, bay leaf, and onion in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, then boil for an additional 10 minutes. Strain the liquid and return it to the pan. Add scallops and cover; let simmer for 3 minutes. Remove scallops and set aside.
4. Slice larger sea scallops into thirds across the grain. Reduce the liquid by one cup. Stir in the butter-flour mixture until it thickens.
5. Incorporate the egg yolk into the cream (or evaporated skim milk for a reduced fat version), add a small amount of hot sauce, then stir back into the sauce.
6. Add lemon juice, cayenne, mushrooms, and scallops to the sauce. Divide among lightly greased scallop shells (6 servings) or individual serving casseroles (if entrees).
7. Melt butter in a small skillet and fry bread crumbs. Mix in Parmesan cheese. Sprinkle over the scallops.
8. Pipe a line of whipped potatoes around the edges of each shell/dish using a pastry tube.
9. Place in a preheated oven at 400 degrees Fahrenheit and broil for 2 to 4 minutes until the topping is golden brown.
